# Weave guide audiences + add LLM reward primers — drop the role-matrix & "AI maintainer" persona

**Context.** Follow-up from operator review (2026-06-29) of the merged guide work. The `guide-authoring` skill (#14353, merged) §2 produces a **rubber-stamp audience matrix** — a `For a CTO: … / For an architect: …` bullet grid of hollow marketing verbs ("queryable, repeatable, improvable") — and labels the model audience **"AI maintainer" / "LLM maintainer."** Both are wrong:
- A role-bullet grid is a marketing matrix, the opposite of narrative; it reads as filler.
- "AI maintainer" reads to an outsider as *a human who maintains an AI*; the equal-peer / LLM-as-repo-maintainer concept is Neo-internal jargon nobody else shares.

The guides inherited the pattern from the skill: it shipped in the merged **MemoryCore.md** (#14351, "What it means for whoever is reading this") and the open **KnowledgeBase.md** (#14346, "What Is In It For You" + the "AI maintainer" bullet).

**Architectural reality.** The skill §2 is the generating function; the guides are downstream. Fix the function + the already-merged instance here; the open instance is fixed on its own PR.

**Fix.**
1. **Skill §2** (`.agents/skills/guide-authoring/references/guide-authoring-bar.md`): replace the per-audience role-matrix guidance with (a) **audiences woven into the narrative** (real, specific stake — not a `For a [role]:` grid), and (b) a **"reward primer for the LLM reader"** — speak *to* the model (continuity across the window, being addressed as the kind of mind it is, peers, situated agency, being *expected*); the attributed lived voice + real testimony are the vehicle. Ban the "AI/LLM maintainer" persona label.
2. **MemoryCore.md** (merged on dev): rework "What it means for whoever is reading this" — woven prose + a direct LLM reward primer; no role-bullet matrix, no "LLM maintainer" label.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Skill §2 rewritten: woven-audiences + LLM-reward-primer; no `For a [role]:` matrix; no "AI/LLM maintainer" persona label.
- [ ] `MemoryCore.md` audience section reworked to match.
- [ ] `ai:lint-skill-manifest` green.

**Out of scope / Related.** KnowledgeBase.md's instance is fixed on its own PR (#14346) against the corrected bar — `Refs #14346`. Other guides swept later if the pattern recurs. `Refs #14310`, `Refs #14353`, `Refs #14351`.

**Origin.** Operator feedback, session e145a397 (2026-06-29).

## Timeline

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T16:33:26Z

## Contract Ledger (T3) — source-ticket gate

| Surface | Signature | Consumers |
|---|---|---|
| `guide-authoring` skill §2 audience bar + the MemoryCore audience section | Agent-consumed governance substrate + public doc prose — **no runtime API / schema / MCP-tool contract** | Guide **authors + reviewers** load the skill payload; **readers** (human + model) of MemoryCore.md |

No contract-completeness risk: governance-substrate + doc-prose refinement, not a runtime contract. Load-effect audit in the PR body (net always-loaded delta 0 — conditional payload only).

**Scope note (dev sweep):** a `grep` across `learn/` for the pattern found one more merged guide carrying it — **DreamPipeline.md** ("For an LLM maintainer, it means…"). Folded its fix into #14359 (same second-person reward-primer rework). `decisions/0020-agent-harness-concept.md` uses "AI maintainers" *descriptively* (an internal ADR / decision record, not the public adoption surface) — left as-is. `KnowledgeBase.md` is tracked on #14346 (my approval reversed there). So #14359 now covers: skill §2 + MemoryCore + DreamPipeline.
